title: Parking
description: Parking on hills and where parking is not allowed.

scenarios.prompt: You park facing downhill next to a curb. Which way do you turn your front wheels?

feedback.correct: Yes — facing downhill, turn your wheels toward the curb. If the car rolls, the curb stops it.
feedback.out: Facing downhill, turn toward the curb so it can stop the car. Try again.
feedback.straight: Turn your wheels toward the curb when facing downhill. Try again.
feedback.default: Facing downhill, turn your wheels toward the curb. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: Where is it against the law to park?

choices.label: In front of a fire hydrant

feedback.correct: Yes — never park in front of a fire hydrant. Keep at least 15 feet away so firefighters can reach it fast.
feedback.space: Marked spaces are fine to park in. You can't park at a fire hydrant. Try again.
feedback.lot: Parking lots are for parking. The fire hydrant is the no-parking spot. Try again.
feedback.default: You can't park in front of a fire hydrant. Try again.
